endingCommit: the commit titled 'Fix pending-changes reliability across controls' that adds this report
userReportedSymptoms: some changed/toggled settings did not show the top pending chip
some did not show the bottom unsaved-changes bar
some did not show the sidebar/page count pill
some required toggling twice before the pending UI appeared

rootCausesFixed.symptom: double toggle required; first change produced no pending UI
rootCausesFixed.cause: controls seeded their initial display from the config line or edit.proposed_value (which for booleans is next_bool_value — a FLIP suggestion, not a value); when live runtime differed (lua-side config, options set outside the parsed file, earlier previews), the first user change applied a value equal to the session's runtime original — a genuine no-op the ledger correctly refused to mark pending; the SECOND toggle then differed and registered
rootCausesFixed.fix: every inline and detail control seeds runtime-first via the new read_runtime_option_live wrapper (~1.6ms per control on this machine), falling back to config raw, then the trusted official default, then the suggestion; uniform css-gap readbacks collapse to shorthand for display (runtime_seed_initial_value/collapse_uniform_gap in src/ui/window.rs)

rootCausesFixed.symptom: color/gradient rows never showed any pending UI; picker Select silently failed (red error class only)
rootCausesFixed.cause: start_runtime_preview_session requires a parsable original, but parse_getoption_value did not know the 'gradient data:' readback prefix this compositor uses for every col.*/gradient option — every color preview session failed at start, so no apply, no pending, nothing

rootCausesFixed.symptom: even with a session, color applies and reverts were rejected by the runtime ('invalid color')
rootCausesFixed.cause: Color and Gradient value kinds fell through to the quoted-string lua rendering; the lua runtime rejects multi-stop or angle-suffixed color STRINGS and requires the table form; reverts re-apply the readback original ('AARRGGBB [AARRGGBB…] Ndeg'), which is exactly such a string
rootCausesFixed.fix: rendered_color_lua_table renders the live-proven { colors = { "rgba(RRGGBBAA)", … }, angle = N } table for both Color and Gradient kinds; color_token_to_rgba normalizes rgba(hex8 RRGGBBAA), rgb(hex6), 0xAARRGGBB, and bare AARRGGBB/RRGGBB hex, so applies AND reverts round-trip; parse_hyprland_color additionally accepts bare hex so swatches render runtime-seeded values and the gradient parser accepts single-stop-plus-angle readbacks

rootCausesFixed.symptom: reverting/discarding some spinner rows failed silently, leaving a stuck pending session that poisoned every later pending readout (found live by the matrix at general.float_gaps)
rootCausesFixed.cause: rows classified Number can be css-gap typed at runtime (float_gaps reads back '1 1 1 1'); the revert re-applies that readback original through the Number renderer, whose f64 parse fails, so the session stayed Active and pending forever
rootCausesFixed.fix: the Number/Percent renderer falls back to the proven css-gap lua table for 2-4 finite-number values (rendered_css_gap_table)

rootCausesFixed.symptom: single-color rows (shadow/glow colors) could not preview at all: seeds fell back to the raw text editor and applies/reverts were rejected (found live by the matrix)
rootCausesFixed.cause: int-typed color options read back as decimal u32 ('int: 1426063360', bits AARRGGBB), which neither the color parser nor the color renderer understood; additionally this compositor rejects the gradient TABLE for color-typed options ('color type requires a color string') while rejecting STRINGS for multi-stop gradient options — the renderer used one form for everything
rootCausesFixed.fix: decimal u32 tokens are accepted as color spellings (executor renderer, semantic equality, and the color-row seed, which converts them to hex for the stop-based control); the renderer picks the grammar by shape — single stop without angle renders as a quoted color string (accepted by both option types), multi-stop or angled values render as the table

rootCausesFixed.symptom: pending state could vanish (or its original silently re-baseline) when a page re-rendered mid-preview
rootCausesFixed.cause: the ledger held weak controller references and every rebuilt control created a fresh controller whose new session captured the ALREADY-PREVIEWED runtime value as its original
rootCausesFixed.fix: ledger entries own their controllers (strong Rc); inline and detail creation paths reuse the row's ledger controller (pending_controller_for_row), so one session and its true original survive re-renders; window-close revert still covers every session

rootCausesFixed.symptom: format differences could mis-classify changes in either direction
rootCausesFixed.cause: pending = last_applied != original as plain strings ('true' vs '1', '0.5' vs '0.500000', rgba() vs bare-hex readback spellings)
rootCausesFixed.fix: pending_changes_ui::values_semantically_equal — boolean synonyms, numeric and css-gap-shorthand equality, color/gradient equality across spellings with missing angle treated as 0deg; fail-closed string comparison otherwise

rootCausesFixed.symptom: initial matrix runs mis-clicked and mis-reported
rootCausesFixed.cause: substring button matching hit the color rows' 'Discard color changes' arrow instead of the bar's Discard; GtkComboBoxText popups are invisible to AT-SPI
rootCausesFixed.fix: exact-name matching for Discard; dropdowns drive the combo's AT-SPI Selection interface (selectChild), which performs a real model selection

bugCountBefore: matrix-reproduced before the fixes: all 22 color/gradient controls could never register pending (sessions failed at start; applies/reverts rejected by the runtime grammar); Number rows with gap-shaped runtime (float_gaps) had stuck unrevertable sessions that cascaded; rows whose runtime diverged from the displayed seed required a double toggle; page re-renders could drop or re-baseline sessions

outsidePendingSupport.scalarRows: 206 visible-but-not-editable rows (blocked high-risk, hardware-gated, not-proven, save-only — save-only rows have no unsaved intermediate state by design; dead-man rows preview through their own supervised confirm flow), plus 3 text-entry rows that are pending-capable but not automatable by the harness (verified via code path shared with other kinds); per-row reasons in the matrix report
outsidePendingSupport.families: hl.animation and hl.curve have real edit paths but use the supervised Preview/Keep/Revert/Save flow with auto-revert — outside the applied-live-until-discarded ledger by design; the other five families are read-only views with no edit path

validation: cargo fmt/check clean; full suite green (see final response for counts); jq clean; git diff --check clean; release build is the binary the matrix drove

safety.runtimeMutated: only via the app's own reversible preview controls during the live matrix; every change discarded per row and cross-checked reverted with getoption
